We are given the task to implement the function `query_course_enrollment_stats` using the sqlgen library.

 We are provided with the table structures and a reference SQL statement.

 Steps:
   a. Connect to the database using `sqlgen::sqlite::connect`.
   b. Create the tables. We can use the `execute` method of the connection to run CREATE TABLE statements.
   c. Use sqlgen's fluent interface to build the query:
        - Start with `select_from` on the Course table, selecting the title and then aggregations on the Enrollment table.
        - Use `left_join` to join the Enrollment table on course_id.
        - Group by course_id and title.
        - Use `to<std::vector<CourseStats>>` to specify the return type.

 However, note that the provided `CourseStats` struct has fields: title, student_count, avg_grade.

 We need to map the selected fields to these structs.

 Important: The reference SQL uses:
        SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) as student_count, AVG(e.grade) as avg_grade
        FROM Course c
        LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id
        GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;

 In sqlgen, we can use the `as` function to alias the aggregation results to match the struct fields.

 Let's break down the query in sqlgen:

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    // Connect to the database
    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
        return {};
    }
    auto conn = *conn_result;

    // Create tables if not exists
    (sqlgen::create_table<Customer> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);
    (sqlgen::create_table<Order> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);
    (sqlgen::create_table<OrderItem> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);
    (sqlgen::create_table<Product> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)(conn);

    // Build the query
    auto query = select_from<Customer>("c.name"_c.as<"product_name">(), 
                                      "o.order_date"_c, 
                                      "oi.quantity"_c)
        | inner_join<Order>("c.customer_id"_c == "o.customer_id"_c)
        | inner_join<OrderItem>("o.order_id"_c == "oi.order_id"_c)
        | inner_join<Product>("oi.product_id"_c == "p.product_id"_c)
        | where("c.customer_id"_c == customer_id)
        | order_by("o.order_date"_c.desc())
        | to<std::vector<PurchaseHistory>>();

    // Execute the query
    auto result = query(conn);
    if (!result) {
        return {};
    }
    return *result;
}
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
};

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Begin transaction
  auto begin_result = conn->begin_transaction();
  if (!begin_result) {
    return false;
  }

  try {
    // Read from_account
    auto from_account_result = read<Account> | where("account_id"_c == from_account_id);
    auto from_accounts = from_account_result(conn).value();
    if (from_accounts.empty()) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    if (from_accounts[0].balance < amount) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Update from_account
    Account updated_from_account = from_accounts[0];
    updated_from_account.balance -= amount;
    auto update_from = update<Account>("balance"_c.set(updated_from_account.balance)) 
                      | where("account_id"_c == from_account_id);
    auto update_from_result = update_from(conn);
    if (!update_from_result) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Read to_account
    auto to_account_result = read<Account> | where("account_id"_c == to_account_id);
    auto to_accounts = to_account_result(conn).value();
    if (to_accounts.empty()) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    Account updated_to_account = to_accounts[0];
    updated_to_account.balance += amount;
    auto update_to = update<Account>("balance"_c.set(updated_to_account.balance)) 
                    | where("account_id"_c == to_account_id);
    auto update_to_result = update_to(conn);
    if (!update_to_result) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Commit transaction
    auto commit_result = conn->commit();
    if (!commit_result) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    return true;

  } catch (...) {
    conn->rollback();
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace transaction_transfer_funds
```</answer>

                std::string trimmed_email = profile.email;
                trimmed_email.erase(trimmed_email.find_last_not_of(" ") + 1);
                trimmed_email.erase(0, trimmed_email.find_first_not_of(" "));
                validated_profiles.push_back(ValidatedProfile{
                    .user_id = profile.user_id,
                    .trimmed_name = trimmed_name,
                    .trimmed_email = trimmed_email,
                    .name_length = trimmed_name.length(),
                    .email_length = trimmed_email.length()
                });
            }
            return validated_profiles;
        }

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

/**
 * description: replace_text_in_notes.
 * This function demonstrates how to replace text in notes using sqlgen update API.
 * It replaces all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in the content column of the Note table.
 * Returns: std::vector<UpdatedNote>.
 */
std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Create update statement: replace "foo" with "bar" in content column
  auto update_stmt = sqlgen::update<Note>(
    sqlgen::col<"content">.set(sqlgen::operations::replace(sqlgen::col<"content">, "foo", "bar"))
  );
  
  // Execute the update
  update_stmt(conn);
  
  // Read the updated notes
  auto read_stmt = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>;
  auto result = read_stmt(conn);
  
  // Transform Note objects to UpdatedNote objects
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  for (const auto& note : result.value()) {
    updated_notes.push_back(UpdatedNote{
      .note_id = note.note_id,
      .updated_content = note.content
    });
  }
  
  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

        namespace analyze_department_employees {

        struct Department {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
          std::string department_name;
          std::string location;
        };

        struct Employee {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
          int department_id;
          std::string first_name;
          std::string last_name;
          double salary;
        };

        struct JoinResult {
          std::optional<std::string> department_name;
          std::optional<std::string> first_name;
          std::optional<std::string> last_name;
          std::optional<double> salary;
        };

        struct JoinAnalysis {
          std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
          std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
        };

        JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
          using namespace sqlgen::literals;

          // Connect to the database
          auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

          // Write the Department and Employee tables
          sqlgen::write(conn, depts);
          sqlgen::write(conn, emps);

          // Define the condition for the joins
          auto condition = "department_id"_d == "department_id"_e;

          // INNER JOIN
          auto inner_query = select_from<Department, "d">(
              "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
              "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
              "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
              "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
          ) | inner_join<Employee, "e">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;

          auto inner_results = inner_query(conn).value();

          // LEFT JOIN

              "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
              "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
              "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
              "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
          ) | left_join<Department, "d">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;

          auto left_results = left_query(conn).value();

          // RIGHT JOIN
          auto right_query = select_from<Employee, "e">(
              "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
              "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
              "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
              "salary"_e.as<"salary">()

              "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
              "salary"_e.as<"salary">()

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string department_name;
  std::string location;
};

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> employee_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
  double salary;
};

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees_impl(const std::vector<Department>& depts, const std::vector<Employee>& emps) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Write the Department and Employee tables
  sqlgen::write(conn, depts);
  sqlgen::write(conn, emps);

  // Define the join condition
  auto condition = "department_id"_d == "department_id"_e;

  // INNER JOIN: Departments with employees
  auto inner_query = select_from<Department, "d">(
      "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
      "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
      "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
      "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
  ) | inner_join<Employee, "e">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
  auto inner_results = inner_query(conn).value();

  // LEFT JOIN: All employees (including those without departments)
  auto left_query = select_from<Employee, "e">(
      "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
      "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
      "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
      "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
  ) | left_join<Department, "d">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
  auto left_results = left_query(conn).value();

  // RIGHT JOIN: All departments (including those without employees)
  auto right_query = select_from<Department, "d">(
      "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
      "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
      "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
      "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
  ) | right_join<Employee, "e">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
  auto right_results = right_query(conn).value();

  // FULL JOIN: All employees and departments
  auto full_query = select_from<Department, "d">(
      "department_name"_d.as<"department_name">(),
      "first_name"_e.as<"first_name">(),
      "last_name"_e.as<"last_name">(),
      "salary"_e.as<"salary">()
  ) | full_join<Employee, "e">(condition) | to<std::vector<JoinResult>>;
  auto full_results = full_query(conn).value();

  return JoinAnalysis{
      .inner_join_results = inner_results,
      .left_join_results = left_results,
      .right_join_results = right_results,
      .full_join_results = full_results
  };
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Build the query for the view
  auto query = select_from<Order>(
                  "order_id"_c, 
                  "customer"_c, 
                  sum("unit_price"_c * "quantity"_c).as<"total">)
              | inner_join<OrderItem, "oi">("order_id"_c == "order_id"_c)
              | group_by("order_id"_c, "customer"_c);

  // Create the view
  auto create_view = create_as<OrderSummary>(query);
  create_view(conn);

  // Read the view
  auto results = (select_from<OrderSummary>() | to<std::vector<OrderSummary>>)(conn);

  return results;
}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
